vocab.stem: All banks were closed on Sunday. He could only hope the ATMs were working so he could ___ some cash.
vocab.trans: 週日銀行都關了,他只能希望提款機還能用,好讓他領一些現金。
vocab.note: 從 ATM『領』現金,故選 withdraw。

vocab.stem: An ___ to driving during rush hour is taking public transportation.
vocab.trans: 尖峰時間開車的一個替代方案是搭乘大眾運輸。
vocab.note: 開車的『替代方案』,用 alternative。

vocab.stem: The hotel is so popular that it doesn't have any room ___ this weekend.
vocab.trans: 這家飯店太受歡迎,這個週末沒有任何空房。

vocab.stem: Millions of Americans are under ___ watches in the Northeast where post-tropical cyclone Debby is rapidly picking up speed.
vocab.trans: 在東北部,數百萬美國人處於龍捲風警戒中,後熱帶氣旋黛比正迅速增強。
vocab.note: 氣旋來襲發布『龍捲風』警戒,用 tornado。

vocab.stem: It is crucial to ___ your time between work, family, and personal hobbies to maintain a happy life.
vocab.trans: 在工作、家庭和個人興趣之間平衡你的時間,對維持快樂生活至關重要。

vocab.stem: My sister ___ salt for sugar, and the birthday cake she made for me turned out to be a disaster.
vocab.trans: 我姊姊把鹽誤當成糖,她為我做的生日蛋糕結果慘不忍睹。
vocab.note: 把鹽『誤認為』糖,用 mistake A for B。

vocab.stem: The mailman will ___ the package to your house tomorrow.
vocab.trans: 郵差明天會把包裹送到你家。

vocab.stem: Eating moon cakes during the Mid-Autumn Festival is an old ___.
vocab.trans: 中秋節吃月餅是個古老的傳統。
vocab.note: 流傳已久的『傳統』,用 tradition。

grammar.q: No one in this world, ___ his closest friend, knows about his big secret.
grammar.note: not even=甚至連…都不。連最好的朋友「都不」知道。even so=即使如此、even if=即使、don't even 為動詞用法,皆不合句構。

grammar.q: Despite ___ incredibly tired, he continued working on the project.
grammar.note: despite 是介系詞,後接動名詞 being。be/been/to be 皆非動名詞,不能接在介系詞後。

grammar.q: We canceled the outdoor party ___ it rained cats and dogs.
grammar.note: because=因為,接原因子句(下大雨)。so=所以(位置錯)、although=雖然、unless=除非,邏輯不合。

grammar.q: While Sarah ___ a book in the library to prepare for her upcoming exam, her phone suddenly rang.
grammar.note: while 引導的背景動作用過去進行式 was reading,搭配突然發生的 rang。read/has read/reads 時態不合。

grammar.q: By this time next year, she ___ her degree.
grammar.note: By+未來時間 搭配未來完成式 will have completed(到那時已完成)。completed/had completed/completes 時態不合。

grammar.q: Thank you ___ all the support you have been giving us.
grammar.note: thank you for+事物=為…感謝,介系詞用 for。of/with/by 皆非此搭配。

grammar.q: Endorphins are ___ the body produces after you get some exercise. These chemicals give you a sense of happiness.
grammar.note: be 動詞後缺先行詞的名詞子句,用 what(=the things that)。how/that/which 需先行詞或語意不合。

cloze.passage: Mr. Lin works at a busy post office in the city. Every morning, he ___(1) early to sort hundreds of letters and parcels. ___(2) the weather is bad, he still delivers the mail on time. Last week, a heavy storm hit the town, and many roads were ___(3) by fallen trees. Mr. Lin had to carry the parcels on foot for several hours. By the end of the day, he ___(4) walking more than twenty kilometers. His manager thanked him ___(5) his hard work and gave him a day off. Mr. Lin felt tired but very proud, because he knew that many people were waiting at home for their letters and packages.
cloze.passageZh: 林先生在城裡一間忙碌的郵局工作。每天早上,他很早起床,分揀好幾百封信和包裹。即使天氣不好,他還是準時把郵件送出去。上週,一場大風暴侵襲鎮上,許多道路被倒下的樹擋住。林先生只好徒步扛著包裹走了好幾個小時。到了一天結束時,他總共走了超過二十公里。他的主管為了他的辛勞向他道謝,還給了他一天假。林先生雖然很累,卻非常驕傲,因為他知道有許多人正在家裡等著他們的信件和包裹。

blanks.note: get up=起床。他每天很早「起床」去分信。give up=放棄、look up=查詢、put up=搭起,都不合。
前後句意相反(天氣不好『卻』照樣送信),用 Although(雖然)。Because=因為、So=所以、Unless=除非,邏輯都不對。
道路是「被」樹擋住,用被動 were blocked。blocking 是主動、to block/block 不能跟在 were 後面當被動。
end up + V-ing=結果落得、最後變成。他最後「總共走了」二十多公里。put up=搭起、made up=編造、gave up=放棄,都不合。
thank somebody for something=為了某事感謝某人,固定搭配 for。to/with/at 都不是 thank 後面接「原因」的介系詞。

reading.passage: Anna had worked as a mail carrier in a small mountain town for over thirty years. Every day, she carried a heavy bag full of letters and packages, walking up and down the steep roads to reach every house. The town had no big delivery trucks, so most of the work had to be done on foot. Many people thought the job was too hard, but Anna never complained. The people in the town loved Anna. For many older residents who lived alone, she was often the only person they saw all day. She did more than just deliver the mail. She checked on the elderly, carried groceries for those who could not walk far, and even read letters aloud for those whose eyes had grown weak. To the people of the town, Anna was not just a mail carrier; she was a friend. One winter, a terrible snowstorm hit the town. The roads were covered with thick ice, and the post office told its workers to stay home for their own safety. But Anna knew that an old man named Mr. Reed was waiting for an important medicine that had been mailed to him. Without telling anyone, she put on her boots and walked two hours through the snow to bring him the package. Mr. Reed was so moved that he cried. When Anna finally retired, the whole town held a party for her. The mayor gave a speech and called her a true hero. Anna smiled and said she had only done her job. But everyone knew that she had given the town much more than letters.
reading.passageZh: 安娜在一個小山城當郵差已經超過三十年。每天,她背著裝滿信件和包裹的沉重袋子,在陡峭的山路上上下下,把郵件送到每一戶人家。鎮上沒有大型投遞卡車,所以大部分工作都得靠雙腳完成。很多人覺得這份工作太辛苦,但安娜從不抱怨。鎮上的人都很愛安娜。對許多獨居的長者來說,她常常是他們一整天唯一見到的人。她做的不只是送信。她會探望老人家、幫走不遠的人提日用品,甚至為視力衰退的人朗讀信件。對鎮上的人而言,安娜不只是郵差,更是朋友。有一年冬天,一場可怕的暴風雪襲擊小鎮。道路結了厚厚的冰,郵局要員工為了自身安全待在家裡。但安娜知道一位叫里德先生的老人正在等一份寄給他的重要藥品。她沒告訴任何人,就穿上靴子,在雪中走了兩小時把包裹送到他手上。里德先生感動得哭了。安娜終於退休時,全鎮為她辦了一場派對。鎮長致詞,稱她是真正的英雄。安娜微笑著說,她只是做好自己的工作而已。但大家都知道,她給這個小鎮的,遠不只是信件。

glossary.zh: 寄出的(mail 的過去分詞)

questions.q: How did Anna usually deliver the mail in the town?
options: By driving a big delivery truck
On foot, walking up and down the roads
By asking other workers to help her
questions.answer: On foot, walking up and down the roads
questions.note: 文章說鎮上沒有大卡車,工作幾乎都靠走路,所以是徒步送信。
questions.locate: The town had no big delivery trucks, so most of the work had to be done on foot.

questions.q: Besides delivering mail, what did Anna do for the older residents?
options: She cooked hot meals for them every night
She checked on them and carried groceries for them
She drove them to the hospital
questions.answer: She checked on them and carried groceries for them
questions.note: 文章列出她會探望老人、幫忙提日用品、朗讀信件,所以選『探望並幫忙提東西』。
questions.locate: She checked on the elderly, carried groceries for those who could not walk far, and even read letters aloud for those whose eyes had grown weak.

questions.q: Why did Anna walk through the snowstorm to Mr. Reed's house?
options: Because the post office ordered her to
Because she wanted to win a prize
Because she knew he was waiting for important medicine
Because she enjoyed walking in the snow
questions.answer: Because she knew he was waiting for important medicine
questions.note: 雖然郵局要大家待在家,但她知道老先生在等重要藥品,所以冒雪去送。這是從上下文推論出的原因。
questions.locate: But Anna knew that an old man named Mr. Reed was waiting for an important medicine that had been mailed to him.

questions.q: What does the word "moved" mean in the story?
questions.answer: deeply touched in his feelings
questions.note: moved 在這裡不是『移動』,而是『被感動』。後面接『哭了』,可知是情感上深受觸動。
questions.locate: Mr. Reed was so moved that he cried.

questions.q: What is the main idea of the story?
options: Anna was paid a lot of money for her hard work
A mail carrier can become a town's true friend through care and devotion
Small mountain towns always have bad weather in winter
Delivering mail on foot is too dangerous to do
questions.answer: A mail carrier can become a town's true friend through care and devotion
questions.note: 整篇在講安娜不只送信,更用關懷付出贏得全鎮的愛與尊敬,這就是主旨。其他選項只是細節或與文意不符。

vocab.stem: The singer made a ___ recovery after the serious car accident. It's hard to believe he's perfectly healthy now.
vocab.trans: 那位歌手在嚴重車禍後奇蹟般地康復,很難相信他現在完全健康。
vocab.note: 重傷後『奇蹟般』康復,用 miraculous。

vocab.stem: In the speech contest, Jane and Bill won first place and third place ___.
vocab.trans: 演講比賽中,珍和比爾分別贏得第一名和第三名。

vocab.stem: There is a high ___ of accidents driving in the storm.
vocab.trans: 在暴風雨中開車發生意外的風險很高。

vocab.stem: Apple Pay and Google Pay offer users the ability to pay digitally in ___ stores, too.
vocab.trans: Apple Pay 和 Google Pay 也讓使用者能在零售商店以數位方式付款。

vocab.stem: Before making an important decision, it is wise to ___ all possible options and their consequences carefully.
vocab.trans: 做重要決定前,明智的做法是仔細審視所有可能的選項及其後果。

vocab.stem: After the earthquake, we find huge cracks along the walls. The house is not ___ and may collapse if another strong quake hits.
vocab.trans: 地震過後,我們發現牆上有巨大裂縫。這房子不穩固,若再來一次強震可能會倒。

vocab.stem: Before starting their road trip, they carefully planned the best ___ to avoid traffic and save time.
vocab.trans: 展開公路旅行前,他們仔細規劃了最佳路線,以避開車流並節省時間。

vocab.stem: Employees in this company are allowed to wear ___ clothes to work such as T-shirts and jeans.
vocab.trans: 這家公司允許員工穿休閒服上班,例如 T 恤和牛仔褲。

grammar.q: Our teacher insisted that we ___ every class on time.
grammar.note: insist that+主詞+(should)原形。堅持我們「準時到」課,用原形 come to。coming/to come/came 皆不合此假設語氣。

grammar.q: During the weekends, he likes hiking in the mountains, swimming in the local pool, and ___ in the park.
grammar.note: 對等連接須平行,前面 hiking、swimming 都是 V-ing,故用 running。to run/run/ran 破壞平行結構。

grammar.q: Susan avoids ___ alone on a long trip because she tends to fall asleep without talking to someone.
grammar.note: avoid+V-ing=避免做。avoid 後接動名詞 driving。drive/to drive/driven 皆不合。

grammar.q: He packed two pairs of trousers and four shirts for the trip. He also brought a jacket ___ the weather turned cold.
grammar.note: in case=以防、以備。多帶外套「以防」變冷。in fact=事實上、likewise=同樣地、for instance=例如,皆不合。

grammar.q: It is dangerous to block an emergency exit in buildings ___ it can prevent people from escaping.
grammar.note: because=因為,接原因子句。堵住逃生門危險「因為」會妨礙逃生。unless=除非、due to 後接名詞、so that=以便,皆不合。

grammar.q: When ___ if he planned to resign from his position, the mayor smiled briefly, avoiding a direct response.
grammar.note: 從屬子句省略後用過去分詞 asked(=When he was asked),因市長「被問」。asking 是主動、ask/asks 形式不合。

grammar.q: My mom gets up early every day ___ she can get our breakfast ready before we leave for school.
grammar.note: so that+子句=以便。媽媽早起「以便」準備早餐。in case=以防、so as to/in order to 後接原形(此處有主詞+can),皆不合。

cloze.passage: Online shopping has become very popular in recent years. Every day, millions of packages are ___(1) from warehouses to homes all over the country. ___(2) this is convenient for shoppers, it also creates a lot of pressure for delivery workers. During holidays, the number of parcels can almost ___(3) in just a few weeks. Many carriers ___(4) working late into the night to make sure every package arrives on time. To solve this problem, some companies are now ___(5) on new machines and electric trucks that can move parcels faster and more safely. Thanks to these efforts, most packages still reach their owners on time, even during the busiest shopping days of the year.
cloze.passageZh: 近年來網路購物變得非常流行。每天有數百萬件包裹從倉庫被運送到全國各地的家庭。雖然這對消費者很方便,卻也帶給投遞人員很大的壓力。每逢假期,包裹數量在短短幾週內幾乎會翻倍。許多投遞員最後都得工作到深夜,才能確保每件包裹準時送達。為了解決這個問題,有些公司現在開始仰賴新型機器和電動卡車,讓包裹運得更快、更安全。多虧這些努力,即使在一年中最忙碌的購物日,大多數包裹仍能準時送到主人手上。

blanks.note: 包裹是「被」運送,用被動 are shipped。shipping 是主動進行、to ship/ship 不能跟在 are 後面構成被動。
前後語意相反(方便『卻』也造成壓力),用 Although(雖然)。Because=因為、Unless=除非、So=所以,邏輯都不合。
假期包裹暴增,數量幾乎「翻倍」double。reduce=減少(意思相反)、cancel=取消、repair=修理,都不合句意。
end up + V-ing=結果落得、最後變成。投遞員「最後都得」工作到深夜。put up=搭起、give up=放棄、look up=查詢,都不合。
rely on=仰賴、依靠。公司開始「仰賴」新機器。refuse=拒絕、remove=移除、repair=修理,字頭像但語意不對,且只有 rely 配 on。

reading.passage: Have you ever wondered what happens to a package after you drop it off at the post office? The journey of a parcel is longer and more complex than most people imagine. First, when you hand your package to the clerk, it is weighed and given a special barcode. This barcode is very important because it allows the parcel to be tracked at every step of its trip. After you pay the postage, your package is placed into a large bin with hundreds of others. Next, trucks carry these bins to a huge sorting center. Inside the center, machines read the barcodes and quickly separate the packages by their destinations. A parcel going to the north of the country is sent to a different line from one going to the south. These machines can sort thousands of packages in a single hour, far faster than any human could. Once the packages are sorted, they are loaded onto trucks, trains, or even planes, depending on how far they must travel. A package sent across the country may fly through the air, while one going to a nearby town simply rides in a small van. Finally, the parcel arrives at a local post office near its destination. A mail carrier picks it up, places it in a delivery vehicle, and brings it to your door. From start to finish, a single package may be touched by many workers and travel hundreds of kilometers. The next time you receive a parcel at your door, take a moment to remember the long and busy journey it has taken just to reach your hands.
reading.passageZh: 你有沒有想過,把包裹寄到郵局後,它接下來會發生什麼事?一件包裹的旅程,比大多數人想像的更長、更複雜。首先,當你把包裹交給櫃員時,它會被秤重,並貼上一個專屬的條碼。這個條碼非常重要,因為它讓包裹在每一個環節都能被追蹤。付完郵資後,你的包裹會和好幾百件包裹一起被放進一個大箱櫃裡。接著,卡車把這些箱櫃載到一個巨大的分揀中心。在中心裡,機器會讀取條碼,迅速依照目的地把包裹分開。要寄到北部的包裹,會被送到和寄往南部不同的輸送線上。這些機器一小時能分揀好幾千件包裹,遠比任何人都快。包裹分好之後,會依照要運送的距離,被裝上卡車、火車,甚至飛機。寄到全國另一端的包裹可能會搭飛機;而送往鄰鎮的包裹,就只是搭一台小貨車。最後,包裹抵達靠近目的地的當地郵局。郵差把它取走,放上投遞車,送到你家門口。從頭到尾,一件包裹可能經過許多人的手,並旅行好幾百公里。下次你在家門口收到包裹時,不妨花點時間,想想它為了送到你手上,經歷了多麼漫長又忙碌的旅程。

glossary.zh: 被秤重(weigh 的過去分詞)
被裝載(load 的過去分詞)

questions.q: What is given to a package right after it is weighed?
questions.answer: A special barcode
questions.note: 文章說交給櫃員後,包裹先被秤重並『貼上專屬條碼』,所以答案是條碼。
questions.locate: First, when you hand your package to the clerk, it is weighed and given a special barcode.

questions.q: What do the machines in the sorting center do?
options: They wrap the packages in paper
They read barcodes and separate packages by destination
They weigh every customer at the door
questions.answer: They read barcodes and separate packages by destination
questions.note: 文章明白寫機器讀條碼、依目的地把包裹分開,所以選這項。
questions.locate: Inside the center, machines read the barcodes and quickly separate the packages by their destinations.

questions.q: Why might a package be sent by plane?
options: Because planes are always the cheapest way
Because it must travel a long distance
Because it is too heavy for a truck
Because the customer paid extra for fun
questions.answer: Because it must travel a long distance
questions.note: 文章說運送方式『取決於要走多遠』,寄到全國另一端的才搭飛機,可推論距離遠才用飛機。
questions.locate: Once the packages are sorted, they are loaded onto trucks, trains, or even planes, depending on how far they must travel.

questions.q: What does the word "destinations" mean in the passage?
options: the places where the packages are going
the prices people pay for postage
the names of the postal workers
questions.answer: the places where the packages are going
questions.note: destination=目的地,也就是包裹『要去的地方』。從『依目的地分開、寄往北部或南部』可看出意思。
questions.locate: Inside the center, machines read the barcodes and quickly separate the packages by their destinations.

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Sending a package by post is far too expensive
A package goes through a long, complex journey before it reaches you
Machines will soon replace all postal workers
Traveling by plane is the fastest way to see a country
questions.answer: A package goes through a long, complex journey before it reaches you
questions.note: 全篇從寄件、秤重、分揀到投遞,說明包裹要經過漫長複雜的旅程才送到你手上,這就是主旨。

vocab.stem: The ___ of people who can ride this elevator is 20, so you have to wait for the next one.
vocab.trans: 這部電梯最多可搭 20 人,所以你得等下一部。
vocab.note: 電梯乘載『上限』20 人,用 maximum。

vocab.stem: Walking in the city after the tragic event, you can see that many pedestrians were weighed down by a heavy sense of ___.
vocab.trans: 悲劇事件後走在城裡,你會看到許多行人被沉重的悲傷壓得喘不過氣。
vocab.note: 悲劇後充滿『悲傷』,用 sorrow。

vocab.stem: This machine doesn't ___ well. It needs repairing.
vocab.trans: 這台機器運作不順,需要修理。

vocab.stem: Giving chocolates as a gift is a custom that smart chocolate companies have spread to ___ their sales.
vocab.trans: 把巧克力當禮物送是一種習俗,聰明的巧克力公司已把它擴大來提升業績。

vocab.stem: When meeting someone new, a warm smile and a friendly ___ can create a positive and lasting first impression.
vocab.trans: 認識新朋友時,溫暖的微笑和友善的舉止能營造正面而持久的第一印象。

vocab.stem: English is a ___ language. Mastering English enables you to travel around the world with no sweat.
vocab.trans: 英語是一種全球性的語言,精通英語能讓你輕鬆環遊世界。

vocab.stem: An oversupply of bananas has caused a great drop in prices. The government is urging purchase to ease the farmers' ___.
vocab.trans: 香蕉供過於求導致價格大跌,政府正呼籲民眾採購以紓解農民的憂慮。

vocab.stem: Bill has changed so much these years that I could hardly ___ him yesterday.
vocab.trans: 比爾這幾年變了好多,我昨天差點認不出他。

grammar.q: I know it's urgent, so I will send the parcel to you ___ it arrives.
grammar.note: as soon as=一…就。包裹「一」到「就」寄。until=直到、unless=除非、in case=以防,邏輯不合。

grammar.q: Not only did she sing a beautiful song, but she also ___ a graceful dance at the party.
grammar.note: Not only...but also... 對等,but also 後接過去式 danced(與前句時態一致)。dance/dances/dancing 形式不合。

grammar.q: Twenty years ago, Mr. Wang bought this house, ___ his two daughters were born.
grammar.note: 非限定關係子句,先行詞 house 為地點,用 where。that 不用於非限定、however 是副詞、which 後句完整不需,皆不合。

grammar.q: Building this bridge has already been too costly in terms of people who ___ while working on it.
grammar.note: 人是「被」害死,用被動完成式 have been killed。be kill 文法錯、been killed 缺助動詞、have killed 是主動,皆不合。

grammar.q: Some people tend to look at the lunar calendar to pick a lucky day ___ they can hold a certain important activity.
grammar.note: 先行詞 day 為時間且後句完整,需「介系詞+關代」on which(=on that day)。while/where/which 在此皆不合。

grammar.q: I'll never forget the day ___ we first met in the library during a heavy rainstorm.
grammar.note: 先行詞 day 為時間且後句完整,用關係副詞 when。which 需當主受詞、where 指地、why 指原因,皆不合。

grammar.q: Mr. Lee used to eat lots of junk food, but recently for the sake of his health, he ___ the bad habit and looks thinner now.
grammar.note: recently 搭配現在完成式,且「主動」戒掉,用 has kicked。was kicked 被動、will kick 未來、had kicked 過去完成,皆不合。

cloze.passage: Today is Mark's first day as a mail carrier. He feels both nervous and excited. Before he ___(1) out, his manager reminded him to check every address carefully. The streets were crowded, ___(2) Mark still managed to deliver most of the letters on time. Around noon, he met an old lady ___(3) was waiting for a parcel from her son abroad. When he handed it to her, she was so happy ___(4) she almost cried. By the end of the day, Mark ___(5) more than thirty kilometers, but he felt proud of his work.
cloze.passageZh: 今天是馬克當郵差的第一天,他既緊張又興奮。出發前,主管提醒他仔細核對每個地址。街道很擁擠,但馬克仍設法把大部分信件準時送達。中午左右,他遇到一位老太太,正在等海外兒子寄來的包裹。當他把包裹交給她時,她高興得幾乎要哭了。到了一天結束,馬克走了超過三十公里,但他對自己的工作感到自豪。

blanks.note: set out=出發。全文為過去式敘述,set 的過去式與原形同形,故用 set。sets 是現在式、setting 是分詞、to set 是不定詞,皆不合。
前後語意相反(街道擁擠『但』仍準時),用對等連接詞 but。so=所以、because=因為,邏輯不合;although 是從屬連接詞,不能放在這個位置連接兩個獨立子句。
先行詞 an old lady 指人且在子句當主詞,用 who。which 指物、whom 是受格、whose 是所有格,皆不合。
so+形容詞+that=如此…以致於。so happy that she almost cried。as/than/which 不能構成此句型。
By the end of the day 表「到某過去時間點為止」,搭配過去完成式 had walked。walked 是單純過去、has walked 是現在完成、walks 是現在式,皆不合。

reading.passage: The post office plays an important role in our daily life. In Taiwan, Chunghwa Post has a history of more than one hundred years. Long before the Internet, the post office was the main way for people to send messages to family and friends far away. A handwritten letter could take several days to arrive, but it carried warm feelings that a quick phone call could not. Today, the post office does much more than deliver letters. People can save money in a postal account, send packages around the world, pay bills, and even buy insurance at the counter. In many small towns where there are no banks, the local post office becomes the center of the community. Older people, in particular, trust the friendly staff who have served them for years. The way people use the post office has changed with technology. Fewer people send personal letters now, but online shopping has made package delivery busier than ever. To keep up, Chunghwa Post has built smart lockers, online tracking systems, and mobile apps. Still, the goal stays the same: to connect people and bring what they need to their door. Some things never change. Behind every parcel and every stamp, there are workers who get up early, rain or shine, to make sure the mail arrives. They are a quiet but important part of the society we live in.
reading.passageZh: 郵局在我們的日常生活中扮演重要角色。在台灣,中華郵政已有超過一百年的歷史。早在網路出現之前,郵局是人們向遠方親友傳遞訊息的主要方式。一封手寫信可能要好幾天才會送到,卻承載著快速電話無法傳達的溫暖情感。如今,郵局做的遠不只是送信。人們可以在郵政帳戶存錢、寄包裹到世界各地、繳費,甚至在櫃台買保險。在許多沒有銀行的小鎮,當地郵局成了社區的中心。尤其是長者,特別信任那些服務他們多年的親切員工。人們使用郵局的方式隨著科技改變。現在較少人寄私人信件,但網購讓包裹投遞比以往更繁忙。為了跟上腳步,中華郵政設置了智慧儲物櫃、線上追蹤系統與行動 App。然而目標始終如一:連結人們,把他們需要的東西送到家門口。有些事永遠不變。在每件包裹與每張郵票背後,都有不論晴雨、早起確保郵件送達的工作者。他們是我們所處社會中安靜卻重要的一部分。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How to send a letter abroad
The role of the post office and how it has changed
The history of the Internet in Taiwan
questions.answer: The role of the post office and how it has changed
questions.note: 全文圍繞郵局的角色,以及它如何隨科技演變,故選此項。其他選項只是文中細節或未提及。
questions.locate: The post office plays an important role in our daily life.

questions.q: According to the passage, what can people do at the post office besides sending letters?
questions.answer: Save money and pay bills
questions.note: 文中明確說可在郵局存錢、繳費、寄包裹、買保險。借書、看醫生、租車皆未提及。
questions.locate: People can save money in a postal account, send packages around the world, pay bills, and even buy insurance.

questions.q: Why is the post office important in many small towns?
options: It is the only place with the Internet
It serves as the center of the community where there are no banks
It is the tallest building in town
questions.answer: It serves as the center of the community where there are no banks
questions.note: 文中說在沒有銀行的小鎮,當地郵局成為社區中心。其餘三項文中皆未提及。
questions.locate: In many small towns where there are no banks, the local post office becomes the center of the community.

questions.q: What can we infer about package delivery today?
options: It is busier because of online shopping
It is now done only by machines
questions.answer: It is busier because of online shopping
questions.note: 文中說網購讓包裹投遞比以往更忙,可推知包裹量增加。並未說完全取代信件或全由機器處理。
questions.locate: online shopping has made package delivery busier than ever.

questions.q: What does the phrase "rain or shine" most likely mean?
options: No matter what the weather is
questions.answer: No matter what the weather is
questions.note: rain or shine 是固定片語,意為「不論晴雨、無論如何」,強調員工風雨無阻。
questions.locate: there are workers who get up early, rain or shine, to make sure the mail arrives.

vocab.stem: What time is it? It is 8:15pm — a ___ after eight at night.
vocab.trans: 現在幾點?晚上八點十五分——八點過一刻。
vocab.note: 8:15 是八點過『一刻鐘』,a quarter after eight。

vocab.stem: At the party, her delightful smile was so ___ that it influenced the mood of everyone present.
vocab.trans: 派對上,她愉快的笑容極具感染力,影響了在場每個人的心情。
vocab.note: 笑容『有感染力』影響全場,用 infectious。

vocab.stem: The value of a diamond ___ on its qualities. Each factor can greatly affect the price.
vocab.trans: 鑽石的價值取決於它的品質,每個因素都會大大影響價格。
vocab.note: 價值『取決於』品質,用 depend on。

vocab.stem: Did you see John in the past two days? I wasn't even ___ that he was ill.
vocab.trans: 你這兩天有看到約翰嗎?我根本不知道他生病了。
vocab.note: 『不知道、未察覺』,用 be aware that。

vocab.stem: In daily life, we must learn to ___ small inconveniences, like waiting in line or dealing with noisy neighbors.
vocab.trans: 在日常生活中,我們必須學會容忍小小的不便,例如排隊或應付吵鬧的鄰居。

vocab.stem: Apartments near MRT stations are popular with those who need to ___ to and from work by public transportation.
vocab.trans: 捷運站附近的公寓很受需要搭乘大眾運輸通勤上下班的人歡迎。

vocab.stem: The foreigner has studied Taiwanese hard in the hope of speaking it ___ and making friends with the locals.
vocab.trans: 這位外國人努力學台語,希望能說得流利並和當地人交朋友。
vocab.note: 說得『流利』,用副詞 fluently;friendly 是形容詞不能修飾動詞。

vocab.stem: The city has one of the world's largest subway systems. The subway system is so ___ that many first-time travelers get lost in it.
vocab.trans: 這座城市擁有世界最大的地鐵系統之一,系統太複雜,許多第一次來的旅客都會迷路。

grammar.q: I have gotten used to ___ tea during these two years.
grammar.note: be/get used to+V-ing=習慣於。此處 to 是介系詞,接動名詞 drinking。drink/完成式皆不合。

grammar.q: I have many foreign friends. Some are from Japan and ___ are all from South Korea.
grammar.note: 把朋友分兩群:一些(Some)…其餘全部(the others)。another=另一個、other 不能單獨當代名詞、others=其他一些(非全部),皆不合。

grammar.q: ___ this game to a no-ranking young player was a heavy blow to his confidence.
grammar.note: 動名詞當主詞:「輸掉」這場比賽是主詞,用 Losing。Lose/Lost 不能當主詞、When losing 是副詞片語,皆不合。

grammar.q: If he had taken my advice, he ___ the mistake. Now he regretted not listening to me.
grammar.note: 與過去事實相反的假設:If 過去完成 + 主句 might have+過去分詞,故 might not have made。其餘結構皆錯。

grammar.q: Valentine's Day, ___ on February 14, has been around for centuries as a celebration of romance and love.
grammar.note: 分詞片語 falling on February 14(=which falls on)修飾 Valentine's Day。falls 會變兩動詞、fallen 是被動不合、which fall 主詞單數應 falls,皆不合。

grammar.q: The students ___ notes quietly during the lecture are the top performers in the class.
grammar.note: 現在分詞 taking notes 修飾 The students(=who are taking)。take/are taking 會多出動詞、who takes 主詞複數應 take,皆不合。

grammar.q: He was heard ___ a love song to his girlfriend.
grammar.note: hear sb V-ing 變被動 be heard V-ing,用 singing 表「被聽到正在唱」。sing(被動後須加 to)、sang 過去式、to singing 錯誤,皆不合。

cloze.passage: More and more people in Taiwan are using smart parcel lockers. These lockers ___(1) at MRT stations, convenience stores, and post offices. When your package arrives, you ___(2) a text message with a special code. All you have to do is enter the code, and the locker door will open ___(3). This service is popular because it lets people pick up their parcels ___(4) they are free, even at midnight. ___(5) the system is convenient, some elderly users still prefer to receive their mail at the counter.
cloze.passageZh: 越來越多台灣人使用智慧包裹櫃。這些櫃子設置在捷運站、便利商店和郵局。當你的包裹送達時,你會收到一封含特殊密碼的簡訊。你只要輸入密碼,櫃門就會自動打開。這項服務很受歡迎,因為它讓人們可以在有空時領取包裹,即使是半夜也行。雖然這套系統很方便,有些年長使用者仍偏好到櫃台領取郵件。

blanks.note: 櫃子「被設置/可見於」各地,用被動 are found。find 是主動、are finding 是進行式、found 缺 be 動詞,皆不合。
When 引導的時間子句用現在式(arrives),主句表未來用 will receive。received 是過去、have received 是現在完成,與「未來收到」不合。
修飾動詞 open 要用副詞 automatically(自動地)。automatic 是形容詞、automate 是動詞、automation 是名詞,皆不能修飾動詞。
語意為「無論何時有空」,用 whenever(每當)。however=然而、whatever=無論什麼、wherever=無論何地,皆不合語意。
前後語意轉折(雖然方便,長者『仍』偏好櫃台),用 Although。Because=因為、Unless=除非、So=所以,邏輯皆不合。

reading.passage: One rainy afternoon, a young clerk named Emma found an old letter at the bottom of a forgotten mailbag. The envelope was yellow and the ink had faded, but she could still read the address. The letter had been written thirty years ago and, for some reason, had never been delivered. Emma felt that she had to finish the job. She searched the old records and found that the house on the envelope had been torn down long ago. The person who sent the letter, a soldier named Mr. Wang, might have passed away. Still, Emma did not give up. She posted a message online, asking if anyone knew the family. A week later, an elderly woman came to the post office. Her hands shook as she opened the letter. It was from her husband, written just before he went abroad to work. He had told her how much he loved her and promised to come home soon. He never did, but his words had finally reached her after all those years. The woman cried, but she also smiled. She thanked Emma again and again, saying that the letter was the best gift she had ever received. Emma realized that her job was about more than moving paper from one place to another. Sometimes, a single letter could carry a whole lifetime of love.
reading.passageZh: 一個下雨的午後,一位名叫艾瑪的年輕櫃員在被遺忘的郵袋底部發現一封舊信。信封泛黃,墨水褪色,但她仍讀得出地址。這封信寫於三十年前,不知何故從未被投遞。艾瑪覺得自己必須完成這件工作。她查閱舊紀錄,發現信封上的房子早已被拆除。寄信人是一位姓王的軍人,或許已經過世。儘管如此,艾瑪沒有放棄。她在網路上發文,詢問是否有人認識這家人。一週後,一位年長的女士來到郵局。她開信時雙手顫抖。信是她先生寫的,就在他出國工作前寫下。他告訴她自己有多愛她,並承諾很快回家。他始終沒能回來,但他的話語在多年後終於送到了她手中。女士哭了,卻也笑了。她一再感謝艾瑪,說這封信是她收過最好的禮物。艾瑪明白,她的工作不只是把紙張從一地搬到另一地。有時候,一封信能承載一輩子的愛。

questions.q: Where did Emma find the old letter?
options: At the bottom of a forgotten mailbag
On the street near the post office
questions.answer: At the bottom of a forgotten mailbag
questions.note: 文章開頭就說艾瑪在被遺忘的郵袋底部發現這封舊信。
questions.locate: Emma found an old letter at the bottom of a forgotten mailbag.

questions.q: What did Emma discover when she searched the old records?
options: The house on the envelope had been torn down
Mr. Wang had become very rich
questions.answer: The house on the envelope had been torn down
questions.note: 文中說她查紀錄後發現信封上的房子早已被拆除。其餘選項文中皆無。
questions.locate: She searched the old records and found that the house on the envelope had been torn down long ago.

questions.q: Who finally received the letter?
questions.answer: Mr. Wang's wife
questions.note: 來郵局的年長女士打開信後得知是「她先生」寫的,可知收信人是王先生的太太。
questions.locate: It was from her husband, written just before he went abroad to work.

questions.q: What can we infer about Mr. Wang?
options: He is still working abroad now
He wrote many letters every year
He had forgotten about his wife
questions.answer: He probably never returned home
questions.note: 文中「He never did」承接前句「promised to come home soon」,意指他始終沒回家,可推論他可能再也沒能回來。
questions.locate: He had told her how much he loved her and promised to come home soon. He never did.

questions.q: What did Emma learn from this experience?
options: Old letters should be thrown away
Her job is only about moving paper
A single letter can carry deep meaning and love
She should look for a new job
questions.answer: A single letter can carry deep meaning and love
questions.note: 結尾點出艾瑪體會到工作不只是搬紙張,一封信能承載一輩子的愛。
questions.locate: Sometimes, a single letter could carry a whole lifetime of love.

vocab.stem: I would like to make an ___ with the dentist to see if there is a cavity in my teeth.
vocab.trans: 我想跟牙醫預約,看看牙齒有沒有蛀洞。
vocab.note: 跟牙醫『預約』,用 make an appointment。

vocab.stem: In the company, he stood out as a ___ employee, consistently punctual and exceptionally responsible with tasks.
vocab.trans: 在公司裡,他是個勤奮的員工,總是準時又對工作格外負責,因而出眾。
vocab.note: 準時又負責是『勤奮』員工,用 diligent。

vocab.stem: Mr. Smith is about to retire from the company, so his colleagues plan a ___ party for him.
vocab.trans: 史密斯先生即將從公司退休,所以同事為他籌辦一場歡送會。
vocab.note: 為退休者辦『歡送』會,用 farewell party。

vocab.stem: We should ___ flights 48 hours before departure to avoid last-minute changes or cancellations.
vocab.trans: 我們應該在起飛前 48 小時確認班機,以避免最後一刻的變動或取消。
vocab.note: 『確認』班機,用 confirm;其餘三字拼法相近但意思不同。

vocab.stem: The sign at the beach ___ that swimming is dangerous when the waves are high.
vocab.trans: 海灘上的告示牌警告:浪大時游泳很危險。

vocab.stem: Taiwan's baseball team beat Japan and ___ its first-ever gold medal in the 2024 WBSC Premier12.
vocab.trans: 台灣棒球隊擊敗日本,奪下 2024 世界棒球十二強賽史上首面金牌。

vocab.stem: I just know there will be a meeting tomorrow, but I don't know the ___ time when it will take place.
vocab.trans: 我只知道明天有個會議,但不知道確切的舉行時間。

vocab.stem: As a ___ of getting into a big argument with his boss, Alex was fired and had to look for a new job.
vocab.trans: 和老闆大吵一架的後果是,艾力克斯被解雇,只好另找新工作。
vocab.note: 作為…的『後果』,用 as a consequence of。

grammar.q: Kristine visits her mom ___.
grammar.note: 「一週兩次」正確說法為 twice a week。two in a week、two times for a week、two times week 皆不合英語慣用法。

grammar.q: Jack taught his students ___ effective ways to improve their English.
grammar.note: ways 是可數複數,用 a few(一些)。a little/a great deal of/an amount of 皆修飾不可數名詞,故不合。

grammar.q: Holding an implicit bias towards a particular social group can determine ___ from that group.
grammar.note: determine 後接名詞子句,語序為「疑問詞+主詞+動詞」:how you treat(someone)。其餘語序錯誤或語意不合。

grammar.q: Bill has been living in Taiwan for 10 years, so he has no difficulty ___ to people in Chinese.
grammar.note: have no difficulty (in) V-ing=做…沒困難,用 talking。talks/to talk/talked 皆不合此固定句型。

grammar.q: When planning to dine in that Michelin-starred restaurant, you ___ make a reservation first. Otherwise, you will find yourself in a long waiting line.
grammar.note: had better+原形=最好。最好先訂位。used to=過去常、would like(需 to)、can't help but=忍不住,皆不合語意。

grammar.q: If you ___, I would take the opportunity and learn as much as possible.
grammar.note: 與現在事實相反的假設,be 動詞一律用 were(If I were you)。am/wasn't/have been 皆不合假設語氣。

grammar.q: This is the school from ___ I graduated.
grammar.note: 「介系詞+關代」from which(=from the school)。that 不可接在介系詞後、what 不可、where 已含介意不能再加 from,故用 which。

cloze.passage: Online shopping is convenient, but it creates a huge amount of packaging waste. Every year, millions of boxes ___(1) away after being used only once. To solve this problem, some companies ___(2) reusable boxes to customers. Customers return the empty boxes, ___(3) are then cleaned and used again. This idea not only reduces waste ___(4) also saves money. Experts say that if more people support this change, the environment ___(5) much cleaner in the future.
cloze.passageZh: 網購很方便,卻製造大量包裝廢棄物。每年有數百萬個箱子只用一次就被丟棄。為了解決這個問題,有些公司提供可重複使用的箱子給顧客。顧客退回空箱,這些箱子接著被清潔後再次使用。這個點子不僅減少廢棄物,也省錢。專家說,如果更多人支持這項改變,未來環境會乾淨許多。

blanks.note: 箱子「被」丟棄,用被動 are thrown(away)。throw 是主動原形、threw 是過去主動、throwing 是分詞,皆不合。
主詞 some companies 為複數,主要動詞用現在式 provide。providing 是分詞、to provide 是不定詞、provided 是過去式(與全文現在式不符),皆不合。
先行詞 the empty boxes 指物且在子句當主詞,用 which。who 指人、where 指地點、what 不能引導關係子句,皆不合。
not only A but also B=不僅 A 而且 B,固定搭配 but。and/or/so 無法與 not only 構成這個句型。
in the future 表未來,用 will be。is 是現在、was 是過去、has been 是現在完成,皆與「未來」不合。

reading.passage: Every time you order something online, it usually arrives wrapped in a box, plastic, and tape. This packaging protects the product, but it also creates a mountain of trash. Studies show that packaging makes up almost a third of all household waste. As online shopping grows, this problem only gets worse. Luckily, companies and shoppers are starting to look for greener choices. Some shops now use boxes made from recycled paper that can be folded flat and sent back. Others have replaced plastic bubble wrap with paper or even mushroom-based materials that break down naturally. A few delivery companies are testing reusable boxes: customers return the empty box, and it is cleaned and used again up to twenty times. Shoppers can help, too. Choosing to receive several items in one box, rather than many separate deliveries, cuts down on waste and saves fuel. Saying no to extra plastic bags and reusing boxes at home also make a difference. Of course, greener packaging is not always cheaper, and some customers worry that their products may be less protected. But experts believe that as more people care about the environment, these new materials will become common and affordable. Small changes, repeated by millions of people, can add up to a cleaner planet.
reading.passageZh: 每次你在網路上下單,東西通常包在箱子、塑膠和膠帶裡送來。這些包裝保護商品,卻也製造出如山的垃圾。研究顯示,包裝幾乎占了所有家庭廢棄物的三分之一。隨著網購成長,這個問題只會更嚴重。所幸,企業與消費者開始尋找更環保的選擇。有些店家現在使用可折平寄回、以回收紙製成的箱子。有些則用紙、甚至能自然分解的菇類材料取代塑膠氣泡布。少數物流公司正在試行可重複使用的箱子:顧客退回空箱,清潔後可再使用多達二十次。消費者也能幫上忙。選擇把多件商品裝在同一箱、而非分成多次寄送,可減少廢棄物並節省燃料。拒絕多餘的塑膠袋、在家重複使用箱子,同樣能帶來改變。當然,環保包裝不一定比較便宜,有些顧客擔心商品保護較差。但專家相信,隨著愈多人在意環境,這些新材料會變得普遍又負擔得起。數百萬人重複的小改變,能累積成一個更乾淨的星球。

questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How to shop online more quickly
Greener ways to reduce packaging waste
Why plastic is cheaper than paper
questions.answer: Greener ways to reduce packaging waste
questions.note: 全文討論網購包裝造成的垃圾,以及更環保的解決方式,故選此項。
questions.locate: companies and shoppers are starting to look for greener choices.

questions.q: According to the passage, how much of household waste is packaging?
questions.answer: Almost a third
questions.note: 文中說研究顯示包裝幾乎占家庭廢棄物的三分之一。
questions.locate: packaging makes up almost a third of all household waste.

questions.q: How many times can the reusable boxes be used?
questions.answer: Up to twenty times
questions.note: 文中提到可重複使用的箱子清潔後可再用多達二十次。
questions.locate: it is cleaned and used again up to twenty times.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a greener choice?
questions.answer: Glass containers
questions.note: 文中提到回收紙箱、菇類材料、可重複使用的箱子,但完全沒提到玻璃容器。
questions.locate: Others have replaced plastic bubble wrap with paper or even mushroom-based materials.

questions.q: What do experts believe about greener packaging in the future?
options: It will become common and affordable
It will only be used by rich people
It will make products break easily
questions.answer: It will become common and affordable
questions.note: 文末專家相信隨著更多人在意環境,新材料會變得普遍又負擔得起。
questions.locate: these new materials will become common and affordable.

vocab.stem: After a 26-hour long flight, Jennifer was totally ___.
vocab.trans: 經過 26 小時的長途飛行,珍妮佛累壞了。
vocab.note: 長途飛行後『精疲力竭』,用 exhausted。

vocab.stem: Regrettably, they decided to ___ the party due to unforeseen circumstances.
vocab.trans: 很遺憾,他們因為無法預料的狀況而決定取消派對。
vocab.note: 因突發狀況『取消』派對,用 cancel。

vocab.stem: It is very difficult to ___ between love and money when you can just have one of them.
vocab.trans: 當你只能擁有其中一個時,要在愛情和金錢之間抉擇非常困難。
vocab.note: 在兩者間『抉擇』,用 decide between。

vocab.stem: The news is warning us about ___ weather; we'd better not go outside right now.
vocab.trans: 新聞正警告我們會有惡劣天氣,我們現在最好別出門。

vocab.stem: Studying every day is an ___ way to remember what you learn in school.
vocab.trans: 每天讀書是記住學校所學的有效方法。

vocab.stem: He worked very hard to ___ his fear of giving a speech in public.
vocab.trans: 他非常努力地克服自己在公開場合演講的恐懼。

vocab.stem: My parents plan to live in the countryside and enjoy a relaxing ___ life when they become old.
vocab.trans: 我父母打算年老時住在鄉下,享受悠閒的鄉村生活。
vocab.note: 『鄉村』生活,用 rural;urban 意思相反。

vocab.stem: It's important to have enough money to live on, but money alone doesn't ___ that you will have a happy life forever.
vocab.trans: 有足夠的錢過生活很重要,但光有錢並不能保證你會永遠幸福。

grammar.q: That award-winning movie ___ by a young Chinese rising star.
grammar.note: 電影是「被」執導,用被動 was directed。directs/directed 為主動、had been directed 時態無依據,皆不合。

grammar.q: The workers were not ___ the result of the meeting.
grammar.note: be satisfied with=對…滿意,固定用法。人用過去分詞 satisfied,介系詞用 with。satisfying=令人滿意的、介系詞 to 皆不合。

grammar.q: ___ the alternator, the battery is the most important component in any car's electrical system.
grammar.note: Aside from=除…之外。除了交流發電機,電瓶最重要。As far as=就…而言、As soon as=一就、Apart in 非正確片語,皆不合。

grammar.q: The tour package offers several activities, ___ meals to ensure a great experience for all travelers.
grammar.note: including=包括(介系詞)。套裝行程「包括」餐點。included 常置名詞後、include 是動詞、to include 不合,皆不對。

grammar.q: A country is believed ___ a comparative advantage over another when it can produce a commodity more cheaply.
grammar.note: be believed to V=被認為…。國家被認為「擁有」比較利益,用 to have。having/that 子句/to have had 在結構或時態上不合。

grammar.q: They considered it impossible ___ the project in two days.
grammar.note: consider it+形容詞+to V,it 為虛受詞,真受詞是不定詞 to finish。finished/had finished/finishing 皆不合此句型。

grammar.q: Jack's dog ran out of his house, ___ by another dog.
grammar.note: 分詞構句,狗「被」追,用過去分詞 chased(=being chased)。was chased 會多動詞、chasing 是主動、to be chased 不合,皆排除。

cloze.passage: Phone and online scams have become a serious problem in Taiwan. Scammers often pretend ___(1) post office workers or delivery companies. They may send you a text message ___(2) says your parcel cannot be delivered, and ask you to click a link. ___(3) you click it, you might give away your personal information. The police remind people never ___(4) money to strangers over the phone. If you are not sure whether a message is real, you ___(5) call the official customer service number to check.
cloze.passageZh: 電話與網路詐騙在台灣已成為嚴重問題。詐騙者常假裝成郵局人員或物流公司。他們可能傳一封簡訊給你,說你的包裹無法投遞,並要你點一個連結。一旦你點下去,可能就洩漏了個人資料。警方提醒民眾,絕不要在電話中把錢交給陌生人。如果你不確定一則訊息是不是真的,你應該打官方客服電話查證。

blanks.note: pretend to be=假裝是,固定接不定詞 to be。be 是原形、being 是動名詞、been 是過去分詞,皆不能直接接在 pretend 後。
先行詞 a text message 指物且在子句當主詞,用 that(亦可 which)。who 指人、what 不能引導關係子句、where 指地點,皆不合。
語意為「一旦你點下去」,用 Once。Unless=除非、Although=雖然、Despite=儘管(且為介系詞需接名詞),皆不合。
remind sb (never) to V=提醒某人(絕不要)做,用不定詞 to give。give 原形、giving 動名詞、gave 過去式,皆不合此句型。
語意為「你『應該』打客服查證」,用 should。needn't=不必、can't=不能、won't=不會,語意皆相反或不合。

reading.passage: Not long ago, most people in Taiwan paid for everything with cash. They carried coins for the bus and bills for the night market. Today, many of them simply tap their phones. Mobile payment has spread quickly, changing the way we shop, eat, and travel. The idea is simple. Instead of handing over cash or a card, you store your payment information on an app. When you buy something, you scan a code or hold your phone near a reader, and the money is sent in a second. Receipts are saved on the phone, so there is no paper to lose. For busy shop owners, this means shorter lines and less trouble counting change. Mobile payment also brings new habits. Some apps give points or small rewards each time you pay, which encourages people to use them again. Others let friends split a bill or send money to each other instantly. During festivals, people even send digital red envelopes through their phones. However, the change is not perfect. Older people and those without smartphones may feel left out. There are also worries about safety: if a phone is lost or hacked, personal money could be at risk. Experts advise users to set strong passwords and check their accounts often. Used carefully, mobile payment can make daily life faster and more convenient.
reading.passageZh: 不久之前,台灣大多數人付任何東西都用現金。他們帶著零錢搭公車、帶著鈔票逛夜市。如今,許多人只要用手機輕輕一刷。行動支付迅速普及,改變了我們購物、用餐和旅行的方式。原理很簡單。你不必交出現金或卡片,而是把付款資訊存在 App 裡。買東西時,你掃描一個條碼或把手機靠近感應機,錢就在一秒內送出。收據存在手機裡,不會弄丟紙本。對忙碌的店家來說,這意味著排隊更短、找零更省事。行動支付也帶來新習慣。有些 App 在你每次付款時給點數或小獎勵,鼓勵人們再次使用。有些則讓朋友分攤帳單或即時互相轉帳。逢年過節,人們甚至透過手機發數位紅包。然而,這項改變並不完美。長者與沒有智慧型手機的人可能感到被排除在外。也有安全疑慮:手機若遺失或被駭,個人金錢可能有風險。專家建議使用者設定強密碼並常檢查帳戶。只要小心使用,行動支付能讓日常生活更快速、更方便。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Cash is safer than mobile payment
How mobile payment is changing daily life in Taiwan
Why night markets only accept coins
How to start a payment app company
questions.answer: How mobile payment is changing daily life in Taiwan
questions.note: 全文介紹行動支付如何普及並改變台灣人的生活方式,故選此項。
questions.locate: Mobile payment has spread quickly, changing the way we shop, eat, and travel.

questions.q: According to the passage, how does mobile payment work?
options: You mail cash to the shop
You scan a code or hold your phone near a reader
You write a check at the counter
You give the shop your password
questions.answer: You scan a code or hold your phone near a reader
questions.note: 文中說買東西時掃條碼或把手機靠近感應機,錢就送出。
questions.locate: you scan a code or hold your phone near a reader, and the money is sent in a second.

questions.q: What benefit does mobile payment bring to shop owners?
options: Shorter lines and less trouble counting change
questions.answer: Shorter lines and less trouble counting change
questions.note: 文中明說對店家而言代表排隊更短、找零更省事。
questions.locate: this means shorter lines and less trouble counting change.

questions.q: Who may feel left out by mobile payment?
options: Older people and those without smartphones
questions.answer: Older people and those without smartphones
questions.note: 文中指出長者與沒有智慧型手機的人可能感到被排除。
questions.locate: Older people and those without smartphones may feel left out.

questions.q: What do experts advise users to do?
options: Set strong passwords and check their accounts often
questions.answer: Set strong passwords and check their accounts often
questions.note: 文末專家建議設定強密碼並常檢查帳戶,以降低風險。
questions.locate: Experts advise users to set strong passwords and check their accounts often.

vocab.stem: That rumor about Sophia's abuse of her dog was ___ because everyone knew she loved animals.
vocab.trans: 關於蘇菲亞虐狗的謠言毫無根據,因為大家都知道她愛動物。
vocab.note: 大家都知道她愛動物,謠言『毫無根據』,用 groundless。

vocab.stem: It will take lots of work to ___ such a big event, so we need more people to help.
vocab.trans: 籌辦這麼大的活動要花很多心力,所以我們需要更多人幫忙。

vocab.stem: In Halloween, people would put on makeups and costumes to look like ___ ghosts or monsters.
vocab.trans: 萬聖節時,人們會化妝、穿戲服,把自己裝扮成可怕的鬼怪。
vocab.note: 裝成『可怕的』鬼怪,用 scary。

vocab.stem: She wore a beautiful dress to the party because she wanted to look stylish and ___.
vocab.trans: 她穿了一件漂亮的洋裝去派對,因為她想看起來時髦又優雅。

vocab.stem: Many of the artist's major works are now on ___ at the museum, attracting thousands of visitors.
vocab.trans: 這位藝術家的許多重要作品現正在博物館展出,吸引數千名訪客。
vocab.note: 作品『展出中』,用 on display。

vocab.stem: Her friends ___ her to try new activities, even if she has to do them solo.
vocab.trans: 她的朋友鼓勵她嘗試新活動,即使她得獨自進行。
vocab.note: 『鼓勵』某人做某事,用 encourage sb to。

vocab.stem: Lily ___ in fright the moment she saw a seagull flying toward her to snatch the food in her hand.
vocab.trans: 莉莉一看到海鷗朝她飛來想搶她手上的食物,就嚇得尖叫。

vocab.stem: After you read my composition, please give me some ___ so that I can improve it.
vocab.trans: 看完我的作文後,請給我一些意見,好讓我可以改進。

grammar.q: That is the house ___ a murderer killed three college students.
grammar.note: 先行詞 house 是地點且後句結構完整,用關係副詞 where。that/which 需在子句當主受詞、who 指人,皆不合。

grammar.q: My decayed tooth is killing me. I cannot wait to have it ___ out.
grammar.note: have+受詞+過去分詞=讓(某物)被處理。牙齒「被拔」,用 pulled。to pull/to be pulled/pulling 皆不合此使役結構。

grammar.q: People often have trouble ___ attention to one thing because their brain wants to drift off.
grammar.note: have trouble (in) V-ing=做…有困難,故用 paying。to pay/pay/paid 皆不合此固定句型。

grammar.q: Every person that you meet ___ something you don't; try to learn from them.
grammar.note: 主詞 Every person 為單數,主要動詞用 knows。to know/know/knowing 皆非正確的單數主要動詞形式。

grammar.q: The teacher, ___ has a lot of experience, is very helpful.
grammar.note: 非限定子句,先行詞 teacher 指人且當主詞,用 who。whom 是受格、which 指物、that 不用於非限定,皆不合。

grammar.q: An e-payment makes ___ possible to do our shopping without the use of checks or cash. What a great convenience!
grammar.note: make+it+形容詞+to V,it 為虛受詞代替後面的不定詞,故用 it。us/one/that 皆不合此虛受詞結構。

grammar.q: At least 33 coastal cities are sinking by over one centimeter per year. That's ___ the rate of current sea level rise.
grammar.note: 倍數+the+名詞:five times the rate(速率的五倍)。fifth time=第五次、five times than 語法錯、five times as much 需接 as,皆不合。

cloze.passage: Many people want to get fit, but they give up after only a few weeks. The key ___(1) building a lasting habit is to start small. Instead of running ten kilometers on the first day, you ___(2) begin with a short walk. Exercising at the same time every day also helps it ___(3) part of your routine. ___(4) you keep going for a month, the habit will feel natural. Remember, being healthy is not about being perfect; it is about ___(5) small efforts every single day.
cloze.passageZh: 許多人想變健康,卻常在幾週後就放棄。養成持久習慣的關鍵是從小處開始。與其第一天就跑十公里,你應該從短程散步開始。每天在同一時間運動,也有助於讓它成為你日常的一部分。如果你持續一個月,這個習慣就會變得自然。記住,健康不是要做到完美,而是每一天都付出小小的努力。

blanks.note: the key to+名詞/動名詞=…的關鍵,固定搭配介系詞 to(the key to building)。of/for/with 皆非此搭配。
語意為「你『應該』從散步開始」,用 should 表建議。must=必須(語氣過強)、can't=不能、needn't=不必,皆不合。
help+受詞+(to) V,受詞後接原形動詞 become。becomes/became/becoming 皆非原形,不合 help 的用法。
語意為「如果你持續一個月」,用 If。Unless=除非、Although=雖然、Despite=儘管,邏輯皆不合。
about 是介系詞,後接動名詞 making(it is about making efforts)。make 原形、to make 不定詞、made 過去分詞,皆不能接在介系詞後。

reading.passage: Many students think that staying up late to study is a good way to get higher grades. In fact, the opposite is often true. Sleep is not wasted time; it is when the brain sorts and stores everything we have learned during the day. Without enough sleep, even the hardest worker will struggle to remember new information. Doctors say that most teenagers need about eight to nine hours of sleep each night. During deep sleep, the body also repairs itself, builds muscle, and strengthens the immune system. People who sleep well tend to feel happier, think more clearly, and get sick less often. On the other hand, those who sleep too little may feel tired, anxious, and unable to focus. Good sleep does not happen by accident. Experts suggest going to bed and waking up at the same time every day, even on weekends. They also warn against looking at bright screens right before bed, because the light tricks the brain into thinking it is still daytime. A dark, quiet, and cool room helps the body fall asleep faster. So the next time you have an important test, do not throw away your sleep for a few more hours of study. A rested mind remembers more and performs better than a tired one ever could.
reading.passageZh: 許多學生以為熬夜讀書是拿高分的好方法。事實上,情況往往相反。睡眠不是浪費時間;它是大腦整理並儲存我們白天所學一切的時候。沒有足夠睡眠,再用功的人也難以記住新資訊。醫生說,大多數青少年每晚需要約八到九小時的睡眠。在深層睡眠中,身體也會自我修復、增長肌肉並強化免疫系統。睡得好的人往往更快樂、思緒更清晰、也較少生病。另一方面,睡太少的人可能感到疲倦、焦慮、無法專注。好的睡眠不是碰運氣。專家建議每天在同一時間就寢與起床,連週末也一樣。他們也告誡不要在睡前看明亮的螢幕,因為光線會讓大腦誤以為還是白天。一個黑暗、安靜、涼爽的房間能幫助身體更快入睡。所以下次有重要考試時,別為了多讀幾小時而犧牲睡眠。休息充足的頭腦比疲累的頭腦記得更多、表現更好。

questions.q: What is the main point of the passage?
options: Studying all night is the best way to get high grades
Sleep helps the brain learn and the body stay healthy
Bright screens are good before bed
questions.answer: Sleep helps the brain learn and the body stay healthy
questions.note: 全文說明睡眠對學習與健康的重要,故選此項;其餘選項與文意相反或未提及。
questions.locate: Sleep is not wasted time; it is when the brain sorts and stores everything we have learned.

questions.q: How many hours of sleep do most teenagers need each night?
questions.answer: About eight to nine hours
questions.note: 文中醫生指出多數青少年每晚需要約八到九小時睡眠。
questions.locate: most teenagers need about eight to nine hours of sleep each night.

questions.q: According to the passage, what happens during deep sleep?
options: The body repairs itself and strengthens the immune system
questions.answer: The body repairs itself and strengthens the immune system
questions.note: 文中說深層睡眠時身體會修復、長肌肉並強化免疫系統。
questions.locate: the body also repairs itself, builds muscle, and strengthens the immune system.

questions.q: What do experts suggest for good sleep?
options: Going to bed at a different time every day
Looking at bright screens before bed
Going to bed and waking up at the same time every day
questions.answer: Going to bed and waking up at the same time every day
questions.note: 專家建議每天同一時間就寢起床,並避免睡前看亮螢幕。
questions.locate: Experts suggest going to bed and waking up at the same time every day, even on weekends.

questions.q: What does the writer suggest you do before an important test?
options: Stay up all night to study
Get enough sleep instead of studying for a few more hours
questions.answer: Get enough sleep instead of studying for a few more hours
questions.note: 文末勸讀者別為了多讀幾小時而犧牲睡眠,因休息好的頭腦表現更佳。
questions.locate: do not throw away your sleep for a few more hours of study.

vocab.stem: You have bought three items and the payment total ___ to NT$3,400.
vocab.trans: 你買了三樣東西,付款總額合計達 3,400 元。
vocab.note: 總額『合計達』某數,用 amount to。

vocab.stem: Everybody in the ___ clapped when the singer finished singing her song.
vocab.trans: 歌手唱完她的歌時,觀眾席裡每個人都鼓掌。

vocab.stem: When the CEO was arrested for his crime, the stock price of the company ___ the next day.
vocab.trans: 當執行長因犯罪被捕,公司的股價隔天就暴跌。
vocab.note: 利空使股價『暴跌』,用 plunged;其餘三個都是上漲,語意相反。

vocab.stem: The doctor put me on a low-salt diet to ___ my blood pressure.
vocab.trans: 醫生要我採低鹽飲食來降低血壓。

vocab.stem: This region ___ some of the most breathtaking landscapes in the country, attracting nature lovers from around the world.
vocab.trans: 這個地區擁有全國最令人嘆為觀止的風景,吸引世界各地的自然愛好者。

vocab.stem: The hotel's ___ offered free drinks, soft music, and a quiet place where guests could read or chat.
vocab.trans: 飯店的交誼廳提供免費飲料、輕柔音樂,還有可供客人閱讀或聊天的安靜空間。

vocab.stem: The teacher is ___ the possibility of effective teaching. So far, she has tried videos and field trips.
vocab.trans: 老師正在探索有效教學的可能性,目前她已嘗試影片和戶外教學。

vocab.stem: Judy is one of my most valued friends; our friendship has ___ over thirty years.
vocab.trans: 茱蒂是我最珍視的朋友之一;我們的友誼已橫跨三十多年。

grammar.q: ___ everybody's effort, the concert was a big success.
grammar.note: Thanks to=多虧。多虧大家努力,演唱會成功。In addition to=除…之外、Because 後接子句、In spite of=儘管(語意相反),皆不合。

grammar.q: ___ the bad weather, the rescue team continued to search day and night for any survivor of the earthquake.
grammar.note: 後接名詞片語 the bad weather,用介系詞 Despite。Though 是連接詞需接子句、As soon as=一就、As long as=只要,皆不合。

grammar.q: I ___ do it unless you help me.
grammar.note: unless=除非。除非你幫忙,否則我「不會」做,用 won't。will 是肯定、don't/didn't 時態不合。

grammar.q: If I ___ more money, I would buy a big house for my parents.
grammar.note: 與現在事實相反的假設:If+過去式, 主句 would+原形,故用 had。have/will have/having 皆不合假設語氣。

grammar.q: The chef ___ a delicious meal for the guests.
grammar.note: 廚師「主動」做菜,用主動 prepared。was/is/has been prepared 都是被動(餐點被準備),主詞是廚師故不合。

grammar.q: Mr. Chen expects his kids to spend time ___ their hobbies rather than use their phones all day.
grammar.note: spend time (in) V-ing=花時間做,用 pursuing。pursue/to pursue/which pursues 皆不合此固定句型。

grammar.q: Sam prefers spending time outside ___ video games inside all day long.
grammar.note: prefer A to B(A、B 為動名詞)=喜歡 A 勝過 B。前為 spending,後用 to playing。than playing/than play(prefer 不接 than)、to play(需 V-ing)皆不合。

cloze.passage: Since the pandemic, working from home ___(1) more and more common. Many companies now let their employees ___(2) at home a few days a week. This change has both good and bad sides. On the one hand, workers save time ___(3) they no longer need to commute. On the other hand, some people find it hard ___(4) work and rest when their home becomes their office. ___(5), most workers agree that having the freedom to choose where they work makes them happier.
cloze.passageZh: 自從疫情以來,在家工作變得越來越普遍。許多公司現在讓員工每週可以有幾天在家工作。這個改變有好有壞。一方面,員工省下通勤時間;另一方面,當家裡變成辦公室,有些人覺得很難區分工作與休息。整體而言,大多數員工同意,能自由選擇工作地點讓他們更快樂。

blanks.note: Since the pandemic 搭配現在完成式 has become(從過去持續至今)。become 原形、becomes 現在式、had become 過去完成,皆不合。
let+受詞+原形動詞=讓某人做,故接原形 work。to work 不定詞、working 分詞、worked 過去式,皆不合 let 的用法。
語意為「因為不必通勤所以省時」,用 because 接原因。although=雖然、unless=除非、so=所以(位置不合),皆不對。
find it hard to V,it 為虛受詞,真受詞是不定詞 to separate。separate 原形、separating 動名詞、separated 過去分詞,皆不合此句型。
語意為「整體而言」做總結,用 Overall。However=然而、Otherwise=否則、Instead=反而,皆不合此處總結語氣。

reading.passage: When Kevin decided to take the postal service exam, many people doubted him. He had left school early to help his family and had not studied English for over ten years. At first, even simple sentences looked like a wall of strange letters. But Kevin had a reason to keep going: a steady job would give his family a better life. Every morning before work, he woke up at five o'clock to memorize twenty new words. During his lunch break, he listened to English while others chatted. At night, even when he was tired, he finished at least one practice test. Slowly, the wall began to fall. Words he once feared became old friends. The road was not smooth. He failed his first practice exam badly and wanted to give up. His younger sister, however, reminded him of how far he had already come. "You are not the same person you were three months ago," she said. Those words pushed him to try again. On the day of the real exam, Kevin felt nervous but ready. When the results came out weeks later, he saw his name on the list of those who had passed. He cried, not because the test was easy, but because he had proved that hard work and a clear goal can beat any starting point.
reading.passageZh: 當凱文決定參加郵局考試時,許多人都不看好他。他為了幫忙家計很早就離開學校,已超過十年沒讀英文。起初,連簡單的句子看起來都像一堵由陌生字母築成的牆。但凱文有堅持下去的理由:一份穩定的工作能讓家人過得更好。每天上班前,他五點起床背二十個新單字。午休時,別人在聊天,他聽英文。晚上即使很累,他至少做完一回模擬試題。慢慢地,那堵牆開始倒下。曾經害怕的字變成了老朋友。這條路並不平順。他第一次模擬考慘敗,曾想放棄。然而,他的妹妹提醒他已經走了多遠。「你已經不是三個月前的那個你了,」她說。這些話推著他再試一次。正式考試那天,凱文很緊張,卻準備好了。幾週後成績出爐,他在金榜上看見自己的名字。他哭了,不是因為考試簡單,而是因為他證明了:努力與明確的目標,能戰勝任何起點。

questions.q: Why did many people doubt Kevin?
options: He was too old to work
He had left school early and had not studied English for over ten years
He did not want a steady job
He had failed the exam many times before
questions.answer: He had left school early and had not studied English for over ten years
questions.note: 文中說凱文很早離校幫家計,已超過十年沒讀英文,因此被人不看好。
questions.locate: He had left school early to help his family and had not studied English for over ten years.

questions.q: How did Kevin study for the exam?
options: He hired a private teacher all day
He woke up early to memorize words and did practice tests at night
questions.answer: He woke up early to memorize words and did practice tests at night
questions.note: 文中描述他清晨五點背單字、午休聽英文、晚上做模擬題。
questions.locate: he woke up at five o'clock to memorize twenty new words ... he finished at least one practice test.

questions.q: Who encouraged Kevin not to give up?
questions.answer: His younger sister
questions.note: 當他想放棄時,是妹妹提醒他已走了多遠並鼓勵他。
questions.locate: His younger sister, however, reminded him of how far he had already come.

questions.q: What does "the wall began to fall" most likely mean?
options: A real wall was knocked down
His difficulty with English was slowly going away
He moved to a new house
questions.answer: His difficulty with English was slowly going away
questions.note: 前文把英文比喻成一堵陌生字母築成的牆,「牆開始倒下」是比喻他的英文困難逐漸消失。
questions.locate: At first, even simple sentences looked like a wall of strange letters ... Slowly, the wall began to fall.

questions.q: What is the main lesson of the story?
options: Exams are always easy if you are lucky
Hard work and a clear goal can overcome a weak start
Only young students can pass exams
questions.answer: Hard work and a clear goal can overcome a weak start
questions.note: 結尾點明:努力與明確目標能戰勝任何起點,正是全文主旨。
questions.locate: he had proved that hard work and a clear goal can beat any starting point.

vocab.stem: The latest ___ of this software is superior to all the previous ones.
vocab.trans: 這套軟體的最新版本優於先前所有版本。

vocab.stem: Peter has a very busy ___ today. He has to attend several meetings and hardly has time for lunch.
vocab.trans: 彼得今天行程很滿,他得參加好幾場會議,幾乎沒時間吃午餐。

vocab.stem: The puppies in the pet shops are so ___ that Mary really wants to have one as a soothing pet.
vocab.trans: 寵物店裡的小狗太可愛了,瑪麗真的好想養一隻來療癒身心。

vocab.stem: I was ___ by how well he looked. It was hard to believe he had been sick just a week ago.
vocab.trans: 他氣色這麼好讓我很驚訝,很難相信他一週前還在生病。
vocab.note: 對他氣色好感到『驚訝』,用 amazed。

vocab.stem: The juice blender broke down after just a few times of use, so Mary returned it to the store and asked for a ___.
vocab.trans: 果汁機用沒幾次就壞了,所以瑪麗把它退回店家要求退款。

vocab.stem: He showed his language proficiency ___ when applying for a customer service job at the airport.
vocab.trans: 他在應徵機場客服工作時,出示了自己的語言能力證書。

vocab.stem: If you want to be happy, do not ___ in the past or worry about the future. Just focus on living fully in the present.
vocab.trans: 如果你想快樂,就別沉湎於過去或擔憂未來,只要專注於充實地活在當下。
vocab.note: 別『沉溺』過去,用 dwell on/in。

vocab.stem: Although air travel has resumed in many parts of the world, airline ___ shortages continue to create flight cancellation and airport chaos.
vocab.trans: 雖然世界許多地方已恢復空中旅行,航空業的人力短缺仍持續造成班機取消和機場混亂。

grammar.q: It was an old friend ___ Tom came across in the park yesterday.
grammar.note: 強調句 It was...that...。被強調的是人 an old friend,用 that(亦可 whom)。which 指物、what 不可、where 指地,皆不合。

grammar.q: The harder you work, ___ to succeed.
grammar.note: 「the+比較級…, the+比較級…」句型,後半要 the more likely you are。possible 不用於「人 be likely to」結構,故排除。

grammar.q: I cannot sing, and she ___.
grammar.note: 空格在 she 之後,須用 not...either 結構:she cannot either=她也不會。neither can 只能用於倒裝「neither can she」,放在 she 後語序錯誤;can either 語意相反;cannot neither 是雙重否定,皆不合。

grammar.q: Neither of the students ___ happy with the contest results.
grammar.note: Neither of+複數 視為單數,動詞用 was。were/are/be 在數或形式上不合。

grammar.q: A: ___ were you born? B: In Taipei.
grammar.note: 答句 In Taipei 是地點,問句用 Where(哪裡)。When 問時間、What 問事物、Why 問原因,皆不合。

grammar.q: As soon as the noisy flock of tourists came into the museum, they ___ by the guard right away.
grammar.note: 遊客「被」警衛制止,用被動 were hushed。hushed/had hushed 主動、were hushing 進行主動,皆不合。

grammar.q: Not only ___ the American student speak Chinese well, but she also understands Taiwanese.
grammar.note: Not only 置句首須倒裝,主詞單數現在式用 does(does...speak)。do 複數、will/did 時態不合語境(她現在會說),皆不合。

cloze.passage: When a typhoon is coming, the post office must change its delivery plans quickly. Managers ___(1) the weather reports closely and decide whether it is safe to send carriers out. If the wind is too strong, deliveries ___(2) until the storm passes. Packages that are marked "urgent" ___(3) handled first once the roads are clear. The staff also call customers ___(4) them know about possible delays. ___(5) careful planning, the post office can keep both its workers and the mail safe during bad weather.
cloze.passageZh: 颱風來襲時,郵局必須迅速調整投遞計畫。主管密切關注氣象報告,決定派郵務士外出是否安全。如果風太強,投遞作業會延後到風暴過去。標示「急件」的包裹會在道路淨空後優先處理。員工也會打電話給顧客,讓他們知道可能的延誤。藉由仔細的規劃,郵局能在惡劣天氣中同時確保員工與郵件的安全。

blanks.note: 主詞 Managers 為複數,現在式動詞用原形 watch。watches 是第三人稱單數、watching 是分詞、watched 是過去式,皆不合。
投遞作業「被」延後,且表未來,用 will be delayed。delay 主動、delaying 分詞、have delayed 主動完成,皆不合。
急件「被」優先處理,需 be 動詞構成被動 are handled;且全文為現在式,故用 are。were 是過去、will/have 無法直接接 handled 構成正確被動。
打電話「為了讓」顧客知道,用不定詞表目的 to let。let 原形、letting 分詞、lets 現在式,皆不合此目的用法。
With careful planning=藉由仔細規劃。Despite=儘管、Without=沒有(語意相反)、Because 需接子句,皆不合。

reading.passage: Ask most people what a post office does, and they will say, "It delivers letters." That answer was complete fifty years ago, but today it tells only a small part of the story. Modern post offices have grown into one-stop centers that serve almost every part of daily life. First, there is banking. Millions of people keep savings accounts at the post office because it is safe, simple, and found in nearly every town. Farmers, students, and retired workers who live far from big banks can still manage their money close to home. Second, the post office helps with official tasks. People can pay their taxes, water bills, and traffic fines at the counter. Some branches even help citizens apply for documents, saving them a long trip to a government office. Third, the post office supports local business. Small online sellers depend on it to ship products quickly and cheaply across the island. Special services, such as cold delivery for fresh fruit and seafood, help farmers sell directly to customers in faraway cities. Finally, in times of disaster, the post office often becomes a channel for donations, sending money and goods to those in need. From a simple letter to a helping hand in hard times, the post office quietly holds the community together in ways most of us rarely notice.
reading.passageZh: 問大多數人郵局是做什麼的,他們會說:「送信。」五十年前這答案很完整,但今天它只說出故事的一小部分。現代郵局已成長為服務日常生活幾乎每個面向的一站式中心。首先是金融。數百萬人在郵局開儲蓄帳戶,因為它安全、簡單,而且幾乎每個鄉鎮都有。住得離大銀行很遠的農民、學生與退休工作者,仍能就近管理自己的錢。其次,郵局協助處理官方事務。人們可以在櫃台繳稅、繳水費和交通罰單。有些分局甚至協助民眾申辦文件,省去到政府機關的長途奔波。第三,郵局支持在地商業。小型網路賣家仰賴它把商品快速又便宜地寄送到全島。冷藏配送等特殊服務,讓農民能把新鮮水果和海鮮直接賣到遠方城市的顧客手中。最後,在災難時刻,郵局常成為捐款的管道,把金錢與物資送給需要的人。從一封簡單的信,到艱難時刻伸出的援手,郵局以多數人很少注意到的方式,默默把社區凝聚在一起。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: The post office only delivers letters
The post office offers many services beyond delivering mail
Banks are better than post offices
People no longer use the post office
questions.answer: The post office offers many services beyond delivering mail
questions.note: 全文列舉郵局在金融、官方事務、商業、賑災等多項服務,說明它遠不只送信。
questions.locate: Modern post offices have grown into one-stop centers that serve almost every part of daily life.

questions.q: Why do millions of people keep savings accounts at the post office?
options: It is safe, simple, and found in nearly every town
It gives free gifts every month
It is only for rich people
questions.answer: It is safe, simple, and found in nearly every town
questions.note: 文中說人們在郵局存錢是因為它安全、簡單且幾乎每鎮都有。
questions.locate: Millions of people keep savings accounts at the post office because it is safe, simple, and found in nearly every town.

questions.q: Which official task can people do at the post office?
options: Pay taxes, water bills, and traffic fines
questions.answer: Pay taxes, water bills, and traffic fines
questions.note: 文中明列可在櫃台繳稅、繳水費、繳交通罰單。
questions.locate: People can pay their taxes, water bills, and traffic fines at the counter.

questions.q: How does cold delivery help farmers?
options: It lets them sell fresh fruit and seafood to customers in faraway cities
questions.answer: It lets them sell fresh fruit and seafood to customers in faraway cities
questions.note: 文中說冷藏配送讓農民把新鮮水果海鮮直接賣到遠方城市。
questions.locate: cold delivery for fresh fruit and seafood, help farmers sell directly to customers in faraway cities.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a post office service?
questions.answer: Teaching English classes
questions.note: 文中提到金融、繳罰單、冷藏配送等,但完全沒提到開英文課。
questions.locate: From a simple letter to a helping hand in hard times, the post office quietly holds the community together.

vocab.stem: To get hired in this ___ industry, you need to have some unique traits that make you stand out from your fellow candidates.
vocab.trans: 想在這個競爭激烈的產業被錄取,你得有讓自己脫穎而出的獨特特質。

vocab.stem: With house prices rising so high, most young people cannot ___ to buy an apartment in Taipei.
vocab.trans: 由於房價漲得太高,大多數年輕人買不起台北的公寓。

vocab.stem: The witness only had a short ___ at the thief's face, so she didn't remember how he looked.
vocab.trans: 目擊者只瞥了小偷的臉一眼,所以不記得他長什麼樣。

vocab.stem: The elderly are more ___ to diseases because our immune systems get weaker as we age.
vocab.trans: 長者較容易染病,因為隨著年齡增長,我們的免疫系統會變弱。
vocab.note: 『易受』疾病侵害,用 be vulnerable to。

vocab.stem: As a great actress, Meryl Streep has received many famous ___ throughout her career in the movie industry.
vocab.trans: 身為傑出的女演員,梅莉史翠普在電影生涯中獲得許多著名獎項。

vocab.stem: Tom put on his ___ to keep his pants from falling down.
vocab.trans: 湯姆繫上皮帶,以免褲子掉下來。
vocab.note: 繫『皮帶』防褲子掉,用 belt。

vocab.stem: The doctor ___ that I exercise at least three times a week in order to stay healthy.
vocab.trans: 醫生建議我每週至少運動三次以保持健康。

vocab.stem: The school administration has promised to strengthen security to ___ the concerns of parents.
vocab.trans: 校方已承諾加強安全,以緩解家長的疑慮。

grammar.q: She ___ all her homework assignments yet, has she?
grammar.note: yet 搭配現在完成式,且附加問句 has she? 顯示主句用 has,故 hasn't finished。其餘時態與附加問句不一致。

grammar.q: Do you know the girl ___ at you?
grammar.note: 現在分詞 smiling 修飾 the girl(=who is smiling)。smiled/smiles 是動詞會造成兩動詞、who smiling 缺 be 動詞,皆不合。

grammar.q: It took me three hours ___ the work.
grammar.note: It takes+人+時間+to V=花(時間)做某事,用不定詞 to do。do/doing/done 皆不合此句型。

grammar.q: ___ I need right now is enough money for the down payment to buy this beautiful apartment.
grammar.note: 名詞子句當主詞且缺先行詞,用 What(=the thing that)。That/Which/Who 皆需先行詞或語意不合。

grammar.q: I think you should keep ___ and never give up.
grammar.note: keep+V-ing=持續做。要「持續嘗試」,用 trying。try/to try/to trying 皆不合 keep 的用法。

grammar.q: In the orientation, the coach gave all the participants two minutes to briefly introduce ___ to the whole class.
grammar.note: 主詞 participants 自我介紹,反身代名詞用 themselves。them 非反身、theirs 所有格、oneself 單數不一致,皆不合。

grammar.q: According to the research reports, the loss of mass from Antarctica's ice shelves has been twice as ___ as previously estimated.
grammar.note: as...as 中間接形容詞當補語,修飾 has been,用 extensive。extensively 是副詞、being extensive(ly) 動名詞片語,皆不合。

cloze.passage: Last summer, a group of students decided to do something good for their town. They ___(1) a plan to clean up the old park near the river. At first, only ten people joined, ___(2) soon the news spread and more than fifty volunteers came to help. They picked up trash, painted the benches, and planted flowers ___(3) the park looked beautiful again. The local people were so grateful ___(4) they brought drinks and snacks for the students. This experience taught the young volunteers that even a small action can make a ___(5) difference in their community.
cloze.passageZh: 去年夏天,一群學生決定為家鄉做點好事。他們想出一個清理河邊老公園的計畫。起初只有十個人加入,但消息很快傳開,超過五十名志工前來幫忙。他們撿垃圾、油漆長椅、種花,讓公園再度變得美麗。當地居民非常感激,還為學生帶來飲料和點心。這次經驗讓這群年輕志工明白:即使是小小的行動,也能為社區帶來真正的改變。

blanks.note: come up with=想出(點子/計畫),過去式 came up with。give up=放棄、put up with=忍受、look up=查詢,皆不合語意。
前後語意轉折(起初只有十人『但』很快變多),用 but。so=所以、because=因為、or=或者,邏輯皆不合。
so that=以便、使得,引導目的或結果(讓公園再度變美)。in case=以防、as if=彷彿、unless=除非,皆不合語意。
so+形容詞+that=如此…以致於(so grateful that they brought...)。as/which/than 不能構成此句型。
修飾名詞 difference 要用形容詞 real(make a real difference=帶來真正的改變)。really 是副詞、realize 是動詞、reality 是名詞,皆不能修飾名詞。

reading.passage: At a small post office in a quiet town, there lived a fat orange cat named Money. No one remembered exactly when he had arrived. One cold winter morning, the staff found him sleeping on a pile of warm parcels, and he simply never left. At first, the manager worried that customers might complain about a cat in a workplace. The opposite happened. People began visiting the post office just to see Money. Children dragged their parents inside, and old men who lived alone came every day to pet him and chat with the staff. The little office became the happiest corner of the town. Money also seemed to know his job. He sat by the counter while people waited in line, calming nervous customers and crying babies. When a clerk was busy, he would jump onto the desk and watch, as if checking that everything was done correctly. Some regular customers even brought him toys and treats. When Money grew old and passed away, the whole town was sad. The staff put a small photo of him by the door, with the words "Our best worker." Visitors still smile when they see it. Money never sorted a single letter, yet he had delivered something just as valuable: warmth, comfort, and a reason for neighbors to come together.
reading.passageZh: 在一個安靜小鎮的小郵局裡,住著一隻名叫「錢錢」的胖橘貓。沒人記得他究竟何時來的。一個寒冷的冬日早晨,員工發現他睡在一疊溫暖的包裹上,從此他就沒再離開。起初,經理擔心顧客會抱怨工作場所有貓。結果恰恰相反。人們開始只為看錢錢而來郵局。孩子們拉著父母進門,獨居的老人每天來摸摸他、和員工聊聊天。這間小郵局成了鎮上最快樂的角落。錢錢似乎也懂自己的工作。人們排隊時他坐在櫃台邊,安撫緊張的顧客和哭鬧的嬰兒。櫃員忙碌時,他會跳上桌子看著,彷彿在檢查一切是否做得正確。一些常客甚至帶玩具和零食給他。當錢錢年老過世時,全鎮都很難過。員工在門邊放了一張他的小照片,寫著「我們最棒的員工」。訪客看到時仍會微笑。錢錢從沒分過一封信,卻送出了同樣珍貴的東西:溫暖、安慰,以及讓鄰里相聚的理由。

questions.q: How did Money come to live at the post office?
options: The manager bought him from a shop
The staff found him sleeping on warm parcels one winter morning
A customer mailed him in a box
He was born inside the office
questions.answer: The staff found him sleeping on warm parcels one winter morning
questions.note: 文中說一個寒冬早晨員工發現他睡在溫暖包裹上,從此沒離開。
questions.locate: the staff found him sleeping on a pile of warm parcels, and he simply never left.

questions.q: What did the manager worry about at first?
options: That Money would eat the letters
That customers might complain about a cat in a workplace
That the office was too small
questions.answer: That customers might complain about a cat in a workplace
questions.note: 起初經理擔心顧客抱怨工作場所有貓,但結果相反。
questions.locate: the manager worried that customers might complain about a cat in a workplace.

questions.q: What did Money do near the counter?
options: He calmed nervous customers and crying babies
questions.answer: He calmed nervous customers and crying babies
questions.note: 文中說人們排隊時他坐在櫃台邊,安撫緊張顧客和哭鬧嬰兒。
questions.locate: calming nervous customers and crying babies.

questions.q: Why did the staff call Money "Our best worker"?
options: He brought warmth and brought neighbors together, even without doing usual work
He earned the most money for the office
He was the oldest worker there
questions.answer: He brought warmth and brought neighbors together, even without doing usual work
questions.note: 結尾說他沒分過一封信,卻送出溫暖、安慰與讓鄰里相聚的理由,因此被稱最棒員工。
questions.locate: Money never sorted a single letter, yet he had delivered something just as valuable.

questions.q: What is the main message of the story?
options: Cats should not be allowed in offices
Someone can be valuable by bringing warmth, even without doing the usual job
Post offices should hire more animals
Old buildings are the happiest places
questions.answer: Someone can be valuable by bringing warmth, even without doing the usual job
questions.note: 整篇藉錢錢的故事說明:即使不做一般工作,也能以溫暖與凝聚力帶來價值。
questions.locate: he had delivered something just as valuable: warmth, comfort, and a reason for neighbors to come together.
